title: Decades-old weeping willow collapses in Boston Public Garden
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 68-year-old willow tree collapsed in Boston Public Garden after exceeding its typical lifespan.
Friends of the Public Garden reported no injuries and noted the tree as previously structurally sound.
Regular maintenance, including pruning and disease management, is conducted to manage the trees' health.

title: These new speed boards in Jamaica Plain, on the Esplanade track cyclists’ speeds, not just cars
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: New speed boards in Jamaica Plain and the Esplanade track both cyclists and vehicles to encourage safer speeds.
The boards gather data on path user volume to help officials assess activity levels on specific routes, according to DCR.
Installations follow resident concerns on cyclist speeds; future boards will focus on monitoring cyclists on paths statewide.

title: Thousands demand Wu address street safety after cyclist killed in crash
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 4,000 people signed a letter urging Boston Mayor Wu to address street safety after Louisa Gag's death.
The Massachusetts Vision Zero Coalition held a vigil for transportation planner Louisa Gag, killed while cycling on Tremont Street.
Mayor Wu announced new safety measures including increased enforcement against vehicles blocking bike lanes and assigned staff to safety projects.

title: Police investigating after man, woman in their 70s found dead in Maine home
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Maine State Police investigate deaths of Carolyn Harris, 77, and Kevin Harris, 75, in West Bath.
The couple was found dead during a welfare check on July 13, conducted by Sagadahoc County deputies.
Autopsies are being conducted; police state there is no threat to public safety.

title: Multiple issues after getting an SUV back from the body shop
author: John Paul
bullets: A reader's 2024 Honda CR-V EX experiences technical issues after repair at a body shop.
Issues include malfunctioning interior lights, automatic locking hatch, and headlights failing to turn on automatically.
Expert suggests recalibrating ADAS and contacting insurance for a possible supplemental claim.

title: Officers shot at during Dorchester traffic stop, police say, prompting outcry from union
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two Boston police officers were shot at during a traffic stop in Dorchester; they escaped injury but the cruiser was damaged.
Suspect Rasiel Joel Carbuccia, 20, was arrested and charged with multiple firearm offenses and assault with a dangerous weapon.
The Boston Police Patrolmen's Association urged public officials to denounce the attack on officers in a public statement.

title: Woman, 25, dead, driver and 3 children hospitalized in crash on I-495 in Andover
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: A 25-year-old woman from Lawrence died in a minivan crash on I-495 in Andover.
The driver and three children from the minivan were hospitalized; their conditions are unknown.
The crash involved a sedan whose driver was not injured; the incident is under investigation.

title: 3 longtime tenants to close or move after Alpha Management buys prominent Allston commercial building
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Alpha Management purchased 149-175 Harvard Ave. in Allston for $12.25 million, impacting longtime tenants.
The Pet Shop, Ritual Arts, and Regeneration Tattoo must close or relocate due to increased rent demands.
Uncertainty grows among Allston residents about future neighborhood changes and the fate of other tenants.

title: Paul Pierce, Kevin Garnett to introduce Doc Rivers at Hall of Fame ceremony
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Doc Rivers to be inducted into the Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame as a coach on August 15.
Paul Pierce and Kevin Garnett selected to introduce Doc Rivers at Hall of Fame ceremony in Springfield.
Rivers ranks sixth on NBA coaching wins list with 1,194 victories, second only to Red Auerbach among Celtics coaches.

title: Jayson Tatum says it’s ‘weird’ that Jaylen Brown is no longer a Celtic
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum expressed that it's 'weird' for him to see former teammate Jaylen Brown traded to the 76ers.
The Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to Philadelphia in exchange for Paul George and multiple draft picks.
Fans showed emotional responses to the trade, with social media tributes and a viral reaction from a young supporter.
